books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.description: The original science-gone-wrong story. A scientist creates life and loses control of it. The actual novel is more philosophical and sad than any adaptation suggests.

books.title: The Art of War
books.author: Sun Tzu
books.description: 2,500-year-old military strategy treatise that has been applied to everything from business to motorsport. Short but dense.

books.title: Flatland: A Romance of Many Dimensions
books.author: Edwin Abbott Abbott
books.description: A square living in a 2D world encounters higher dimensions. A meditation on perception, mathematics, and Victorian class hierarchy.

books.title: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea
books.author: Jules Verne
books.description: Captain Nemo's submarine Nautilus and the first great sci-fi submarine adventure. Verne's technical obsessiveness is part of the charm.

books.title: Around the World in 80 Days
books.author: Jules Verne
books.description: Phileas Fogg bets he can circumnavigate the globe in 80 days using every mode of transport available. Tight, propulsive, and endlessly adapted.

books.title: The War of the Worlds
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.description: Martians invade England. The original alien invasion story and a biting critique of British imperialism. Surprisingly short.

books.title: The Time Machine
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.description: The novella that invented modern time travel fiction. The far future Wells imagines is deeply weird and unsettling.

books.title: The Invisible Man
books.author: H.G. Wells
books.description: A scientist makes himself invisible and gradually loses his mind. Wells exploring hubris and the limits of science.

books.title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court
books.author: Mark Twain
books.description: A 19th century American engineer is knocked back to Arthurian England. Twain satirizing both medieval romance and modern industrial capitalism with equal venom.

books.title: The Mysterious Island
books.author: Jules Verne
books.description: Castaways on a remote island apply Victorian engineering to survive. The practical science of building civilization from scratch.

wikipedia.title: Formula One
wikipedia.description: The complete Wikipedia article on Formula 1 racing — history, governance, technical regulations, and more.

wikipedia.title: 2026 Formula One World Championship
wikipedia.description: The current season — drivers, teams, calendar, and results so far.

wikipedia.title: Miami International Autodrome
wikipedia.description: The circuit around Hard Rock Stadium — layout, history, and statistics.

wikipedia.title: Hard Rock Stadium
wikipedia.description: The Miami Dolphins' home and the centerpiece of the Miami GP complex.

wikipedia.title: Miami Gardens
wikipedia.description: The city where you are right now — incorporated 2003, largest majority-Black city in Florida.

wikipedia.title: Miami
wikipedia.description: The city itself — history, culture, economy, and geography.

wikipedia.title: George Russell
wikipedia.description: Mercedes driver, 2022 Brazilian GP winner. #63.

wikipedia.title: Lewis Hamilton
wikipedia.description: 7-time World Champion, now at Ferrari. The most decorated driver in F1 history.

wikipedia.title: Max Verstappen
wikipedia.description: 4-time World Champion, Red Bull Racing. Dominant force of the recent era.

wikipedia.title: Lando Norris
wikipedia.description: McLaren driver and 2024/2025 Miami GP winner. #4.

wikipedia.title: Charles Leclerc
wikipedia.description: Ferrari driver, Monaco native. Talented qualifier, multiple race wins.

wikipedia.title: McLaren
wikipedia.description: One of F1's most storied teams — history, championships, current car.

wikipedia.title: Ferrari in Formula One
wikipedia.description: The Scuderia — the only team to compete in every F1 season since 1950.

wikipedia.title: Mercedes-Benz in Formula One
wikipedia.description: The dominant force of 2014–2021 and their ongoing journey back to the front.

wikipedia.title: Red Bull Racing
wikipedia.description: From energy drink backroom team to championship factory. Verstappen's home.

wikipedia.title: Williams Racing
wikipedia.description: The most successful privateer team in F1 history, founded by Frank Williams.

wikipedia.title: Drag Reduction System (DRS)
wikipedia.description: How the moveable rear wing works and its role in enabling overtaking.

wikipedia.title: Kinetic Energy Recovery System (KERS/ERS)
wikipedia.description: How F1's hybrid energy recovery systems work.

wikipedia.title: Formula One Car
wikipedia.description: Complete overview of F1 car components, regulations, and engineering.

wikipedia.title: Artificial Intelligence
wikipedia.description: The Wikipedia article on AI — history, approaches, and current state of the field.

wikipedia.title: Large Language Model
wikipedia.description: How LLMs work, training, capabilities, and limitations.

wikipedia.title: Machine Learning
wikipedia.description: The field underpinning modern AI — algorithms, applications, and history.

wikipedia.title: FIA Formula 2 Championship
wikipedia.description: The feeder series directly below F1. Some of these drivers will be in F1 cars within a few years.

wikipedia.title: Porsche Carrera Cup
wikipedia.description: Single-make Porsche 911 GT3 Cup racing series — also on the Miami GP support bill.

wikipedia.title: History of Formula One
wikipedia.description: From the 1950 British Grand Prix to today — the full arc of the world's most elite motorsport.

wikipedia.title: Aerodynamics
wikipedia.description: The physics of airflow — the science that governs everything that happens on an F1 circuit.

wikipedia.title: Fernando Alonso
wikipedia.description: Two-time World Champion, one of the most decorated drivers in F1 history. Renault, McLaren, Ferrari, and Aston Martin stints.

wikipedia.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
wikipedia.description: Spanish driver, 2024 Ferrari race winner. Son of rally legend Carlos Sainz Sr. Moved to Williams for 2025.

wikipedia.title: Alexander Albon
wikipedia.description: Thai-British Williams driver. Red Bull junior alumnus, returned to the grid after a sabbatical year.

wikipedia.title: Sergio Pérez
wikipedia.description: Mexican driver, former Red Bull Racing #2. Multiple GP wins. Departed Red Bull after 2024.

wikipedia.title: Valtteri Bottas
wikipedia.description: Finnish driver, former Mercedes and Alfa Romeo/Sauber. Known for consistency and strong qualifying pace.

wikipedia.title: Esteban Ocon
wikipedia.description: French Alpine driver who moved to Haas for 2025. Alpine race winner from 2021 Hungarian GP.

wikipedia.title: Oliver Bearman
wikipedia.description: British Haas driver, Ferrari junior. Made his F1 debut mid-2024 replacing Sainz at Ferrari.

wikipedia.title: Nico Hülkenberg
wikipedia.description: German veteran, longest-serving driver without a podium. Moved to Sauber/Kick for 2025.

wikipedia.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
wikipedia.description: Brazilian F2 champion 2024, Sauber/Kick driver for 2025. McLaren junior academy product.

wikipedia.title: Lance Stroll
wikipedia.description: Canadian Aston Martin driver, son of team owner Lawrence Stroll. Multiple podium finishes.

wikipedia.title: Pierre Gasly
wikipedia.description: French Alpine driver. Former Red Bull driver, 2020 Italian GP winner in an AlphaTauri upset.

wikipedia.title: Franco Colapinto
wikipedia.description: Argentine driver, exciting Williams substitute mid-2024. Part of the 2025 Alpine lineup.

wikipedia.title: Isack Hadjar
wikipedia.description: French-Algerian Red Bull junior, debuted in F1 2025 with Racing Bulls.

wikipedia.title: Liam Lawson
wikipedia.description: New Zealand Red Bull driver. Multiple substitute appearances before securing a 2025 race seat.

wikipedia.title: Arvid Lindblad
wikipedia.description: British-Swedish McLaren junior driver. One of the most hyped prospects in the junior ranks.

wikipedia.title: Oscar Piastri
wikipedia.description: Australian McLaren driver, 2025 title contender. 2021 F2 and 2022 F3 champion. Smooth, rapid development.

wikipedia.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
wikipedia.description: Italian Mercedes driver for 2025, replacing Lewis Hamilton. One of the most hyped junior prospects in years.

wikipedia.title: 24 Hours of Le Mans
wikipedia.description: The greatest endurance race in the world — 24 hours on the Circuit de la Sarthe. History, records, famous battles.

wikipedia.title: Indianapolis 500
wikipedia.description: The 500-mile oval race at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway. Part of the original Triple Crown of Motorsport.

wikipedia.title: NASCAR
wikipedia.description: America's most popular domestic racing series. Stock car racing from the Daytona 500 to Talladega.

wikipedia.title: Ayrton Senna
wikipedia.description: Three-time World Champion, the most beloved F1 driver in history. His 1994 death at Imola changed the sport forever.

wikipedia.title: Michael Schumacher
wikipedia.description: Seven-time World Champion, the record-holder until Hamilton. Dominated F1 from 1994–2004.

wikipedia.title: Niki Lauda
wikipedia.description: Austrian three-time champion. Survived the 1976 Nürburgring fire and returned to racing within weeks.

wikipedia.title: Formula One Racing
wikipedia.description: Overview of Formula One racing — the mechanics, rules, and culture of the sport.

wikipedia.title: 1994 San Marino Grand Prix
wikipedia.description: The darkest weekend in F1 history. Ratzenberger on Saturday, Senna on Sunday. The turning point for safety.

wikipedia.title: 2021 Abu Dhabi Grand Prix
wikipedia.description: The controversial season finale where Verstappen overtook Hamilton on the final lap under disputed safety car procedures.

wikipedia.title: Monaco Grand Prix
wikipedia.description: The crown jewel of the calendar. The narrow streets of the principality, held since 1929.

wikipedia.title: Everglades
wikipedia.description: The slow-moving river of grass that covers much of southern Florida. Unique ecosystem, endangered.

wikipedia.title: Key Biscayne
wikipedia.description: Barrier island south of Miami Beach. Home to Crandon Park and the former Homestead circuit.

wikipedia.title: Wynwood
wikipedia.description: Miami's arts and food district. Street art, galleries, breweries, and restaurants in a former warehouse district.

wikipedia.title: Little Havana
wikipedia.description: The heart of Cuban Miami. Calle Ocho, Versailles Restaurant, dominos, café cubano.

wikipedia.title: South Beach
wikipedia.description: The iconic beach district on Miami Beach. Art Deco architecture, nightlife, Ocean Drive.

wikipedia.title: Miami Beach Art Deco Historic District
wikipedia.description: The world's largest collection of Art Deco architecture. National Historic Landmark.

wikipedia.title: PortMiami
wikipedia.description: The cruise capital of the world. One of the busiest cruise and cargo ports in the United States.

wikipedia.title: Biscayne Bay
wikipedia.description: The shallow estuary between Miami and Miami Beach. Home to manatees, dolphins, and mangroves.

wikipedia.title: Carbon Fiber Reinforced Polymer
wikipedia.description: The material that F1 monocoques are made from. Lightweight, incredibly strong, expensive to produce.

wikipedia.title: Computational Fluid Dynamics
wikipedia.description: The simulation technique that F1 teams use to model airflow and optimize aerodynamics without a wind tunnel.

wikipedia.title: Telemetry
wikipedia.description: Real-time data transmission — how F1 cars send 100+ GB of performance data per race weekend.

wikipedia.title: Lithium-ion Battery
wikipedia.description: The battery technology powering F1's ERS and all modern EVs.

wikipedia.title: Electric Motor
wikipedia.description: How electric motors work — the technology behind F1's MGU-K and the EV revolution.

wikipedia.title: Turbocharger
wikipedia.description: How turbochargers work — critical to understanding F1's current 1.6L V6 power units.

wikipedia.title: Monocoque
wikipedia.description: The single-shell structural design at the heart of every F1 car's safety cell.

wikipedia.title: Wind Tunnel
wikipedia.description: How teams use scale models and full-size wind tunnels to develop aerodynamic packages.

wikipedia.title: Computer Vision
wikipedia.description: The field of AI that teaches machines to interpret visual information. Used in autonomous vehicles and driver tracking.

wikipedia.title: Reinforcement Learning
wikipedia.description: The AI technique where agents learn by trial and reward. Used in game-playing AI and robot control.

wikipedia.title: Neural Network (Machine Learning)
wikipedia.description: The fundamental architecture behind modern deep learning — inspired by (but very different from) biological neurons.

wikipedia.title: GPT-4
wikipedia.description: OpenAI's large language model — the architecture that sparked the current AI boom.

wikipedia.title: Transformer (Deep Learning Architecture)
wikipedia.description: The "Attention Is All You Need" architecture that underlies GPT, BERT, and virtually all modern LLMs.

wikipedia.title: Convolutional Neural Network
wikipedia.description: The architecture that powers image recognition — the backbone of computer vision since AlexNet in 2012.

wikipedia.title: Natural Language Processing
wikipedia.description: The field of AI concerned with language understanding and generation.

wikipedia.title: SpaceX
wikipedia.description: Elon Musk's rocket company that revolutionized reusable rocketry and commercial space access.

wikipedia.title: James Webb Space Telescope
wikipedia.description: The most powerful space telescope ever built. Images from JWST have rewritten our understanding of the early universe.

wikipedia.title: CERN
wikipedia.description: The European nuclear research organization. Home of the Large Hadron Collider and the birthplace of the World Wide Web.

wikipedia.title: Raspberry Pi
wikipedia.description: The British single-board computer that democratized hardware hacking. The backbone of countless homelab projects.

articles.description: The complete safety evolution from 1950 to today — Senna's death, the Halo's introduction, and current safety technology.

articles.description: The crypto-F1 relationship, why it happened in 2021, the FTX collapse, current state of crypto sponsorship.

articles.description: How FE and F1 approach engineering differently, where they borrow from each other, and the 2026 convergence.

articles.description: Cuban food, Haitian food, seafood, Wynwood restaurants, craft beer, and how to actually eat well in Miami.
